questions.question: The subset of super key is a candidate key under what condition?

options.a: No proper subset is a super key
options.d: Each subset is a super key

questions.correct_answer_text: No proper subset is a super key

An attribute A of datatype varchar(20) has the value 'Avi'. The attribute B of datatype char(20) has value 'Reed'. Here attribute A has ___ spaces and attribute B has ___ spaces.
questions.note: varchar stores only actual chars (3 for 'Avi'), char pads to full width (20 for 'Reed' in char(20))

An attribute in a relation is a foreign key if the _______ key from one relation is used as an attribute in that relation.

Table programmer has 10 records. It has a non-NULL SALARY column which is also unique. What will be the output: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM programmer WHERE SALARY > ALL (SELECT SALARY FROM programmer)?
questions.note: No salary can be greater than ALL salaries (i.e. greater than the maximum), so result is 0

questions.question: In SQL, aggregate functions can be used in the select list or the ___ clause. They cannot be used in a ___ clause.
Write a SQL query to find the employee whose average salary is more than 20000 in the same department.
questions.answer: SELECT department_id, AVG(salary) FROM employee GROUP BY department_id HAVING AVG(salary) > 20000;

questions.question: Write a query to select top 10% row from grade table.
questions.answer: SELECT TOP 10 PERCENT * FROM grade; -- (SQL Server) OR SELECT * FROM grade LIMIT (SELECT CEIL(0.1 * COUNT(*)) FROM grade); -- (MySQL)

questions.question: Given: Customer(cust_id, Name, Address, Sales_id), Order(Order_id, cust_id, Date, Sales_id), Salesman(Sales_id, commision). i) Find customer details for salesmen with commission > 12%. ii) Count salesmen by order_id and date.
questions.answer: i) SELECT c.* FROM Customer c, Order o, Salesman s WHERE c.cust_id=o.cust_id AND o.Sales_id=s.Sales_id AND s.commision > 0.12; ii) SELECT o.Order_id, o.Date, COUNT(s.Sales_id) FROM Order o, Salesman s WHERE o.Sales_id=s.Sales_id GROUP BY o.Order_id, o.Date;

questions.question: Which one is not a unary operation in relational algebra?
questions.note: Union is a binary operation (needs two relations). Select, Project, Rename are unary.

options.b: Data elements in the database can be modified by changing the data dictionary

questions.question: An instance of relational schema R(A, B, C) has distinct values of A, including NULL values. Which one of the following is true?

questions.question: The natural join is equal to:

options.b: Combination of Union and Cartesian product
options.c: Combination of selection and Cartesian product
options.d: Combination of projection and Cartesian product

questions.correct_answer_text: Combination of selection and Cartesian product

questions.question: In SQL the statement select * from R, S is equivalent to

options.a: Select * from R natural join S
options.b: Select * from R union join S
options.c: Select * from R cross join S
options.d: Select * from R inner join S

questions.correct_answer_text: Select * from R cross join S
